name: Apollo 20 Secret Mission
description: The Apollo 20 Secret Mission is a conspiracy theory claiming that a joint American-Soviet mission launched in 1976 to the far side of the Moon, specifically the Delporte-Izsak region, to investigate a large, abandoned alien spacecraft and a humanoid female being found inside. The theory originated in the mid-2000s with videos and images posted on YouTube by a user named 'retiredafb', identified as William Rutledge, who claimed to be a former astronaut involved in the mission. Proponents suggest the mission was highly classified and aimed at recovering alien technology. Mainstream scientific and historical communities dismiss the Apollo 20 claims as a hoax, citing inconsistencies in Rutledge's story, the lack of corroborating evidence from official sources, and the implausibility of the technology and logistics involved in such a clandestine mission. The supposed evidence primarily consists of low-resolution videos and images, often of questionable authenticity, which are interpreted by believers as proof of the alien spacecraft and its occupant. Skeptics point to the ease with which such imagery can be faked using modern computer graphics and the lack of any official documentation supporting the mission's existence.
subtitle: Alleged secret lunar expedition to alien craft

claims.claim: A joint US-Soviet Apollo 20 mission occurred in 1976.
claims.evidence: Videos and images posted online by William Rutledge claiming to be an astronaut on the mission.
claims.criticism: No official records or corroborating evidence support the existence of Apollo 20. NASA denies any such mission.

claims.claim: The mission's objective was to investigate a large alien spacecraft on the far side of the Moon.
claims.evidence: Videos showing a cigar-shaped object on the lunar surface, purportedly the alien spacecraft.
claims.criticism: The videos are of low quality and easily faked. The object's appearance can be attributed to pareidolia or deliberate fabrication.

claims.claim: An alien humanoid female was discovered inside the spacecraft, in a state of suspended animation.
claims.evidence: Videos showing a female humanoid figure with cables attached, claimed to be the alien 'Mona Lisa'.
claims.criticism: The humanoid figure's appearance is inconsistent with known alien morphology. The video is likely a hoax, potentially using a modified mannequin.

keyFigures.name: William Rutledge
keyFigures.role: Claimed to be an Apollo 20 astronaut and source of the videos and images.

keyFigures.name: José Escamilla
keyFigures.role: UFO researcher who promoted Rutledge's claims and analyzed the videos.

keyFigures.name: Philip Plait
keyFigures.role: Astronomer and science communicator who debunked the Apollo 20 claims on his 'Bad Astronomy' blog.

timeline.event: Alleged Apollo 20 mission takes place (according to the theory).
William Rutledge begins posting videos and images related to Apollo 20 on YouTube.
The Apollo 20 conspiracy theory gains traction online, sparking debate and analysis.

sources.author: Various online forums and websites
sources.work: Discussions and analyses of the Apollo 20 theory
sources.title: Various online forums and websites
